journal_entry.description: Meal for factory production staff - manufacturing overhead
journal_entry.rationale: The meal provided to factory production staff is a manufacturing overhead cost. Under IFRS, manufacturing overhead is accumulated in Work in Progress (WIP) inventory as part of product costs, not expensed immediately. The debit tuple (1,0,0,0,0,0) correctly indicates an asset increase (WIP inventory), and the credit tuple (1,0,0,0,0,0) correctly indicates a liability increase (credit card payable). This follows the matching principle where manufacturing costs are capitalized to inventory and expensed only when the goods are sold.

output.reason: The meal cost is manufacturing overhead, which under IFRS is capitalized as part of inventory (Work-in-Process or Finished Goods). Manufacturing overhead becomes part of the cost of inventory, which is an asset. Therefore, this is 1 asset increase (inventory or WIP). The credit side would be to accounts payable/credit card liability.

debit_classifier.reason: The meal cost is manufacturing overhead, which under IFRS is capitalized as part of inventory (Work-in-Process or Finished Goods). Manufacturing overhead becomes part of the cost of inventory, which is an asset. Therefore, this is 1 asset increase (inventory or WIP). The credit side would be to accounts payable/credit card liability.

output.reason: The meal was paid using a corporate credit card, which creates or increases a liability (credit card payable). This is a liability increase on the credit side. The debit side would record the manufacturing overhead expense, but we only classify credits here.

credit_classifier.reason: The meal was paid using a corporate credit card, which creates or increases a liability (credit card payable). This is a liability increase on the credit side. The debit side would record the manufacturing overhead expense, but we only classify credits here.
